title: Values in cells actually not formatted as well
author: frenzymind
body:
Hello.
I set values for row and set format like this:
```
    const row = worksheet.getRow(...);
    row.numFmt = 'hh:mm';
    row.values = userRec;
```
userRec is array of strings that represents time: ["10:51","10:56","10:46"]
When I open created document, I see my data, formatted as I want, but:
I want see sum of times, I type formula in cell =SUM(C7:D7) and see 00:00
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33678873/73725007-84985200-4767-11ea-8c1e-4fc9898a830a.png)
In LibreOffice when I look in cell with tiimes I see there is symbol ' infront of value, and when I remove it and hit enter - formula start to work. I have to do it with every cell.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33678873/73725345-291a9400-4768-11ea-8bf4-3f1f1c1e1245.png)
In MS Office I have no symbol ' infront of value, but formula still don't work, I have to click cell and hit enter - then formula works.
That is how it looks after:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33678873/73725783-1785bc00-4769-11ea-84cb-0c303f91f0b6.png)

In both cases cell has right format "hh:mm" and time type.
How can I fix this ?

comments.author: arbitora
comments.body:
Same problem here.
We'd like duration support.

comments.author: enisz
comments.body:
Same issue here. I have a test repo here: [enisz/exceljs-numFmt](https://github.com/enisz/exceljs-numFmt). Cannot calculate duration unless I'm clicking over all of the cells.

![issue](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33585488/83992172-9aa16380-a94f-11ea-8b8f-16f9aeb6500b.gif)

Pretty old issue, but hopefully someone can find at least a workaround.

@enisz

comments.author: Farzad6049
tvoloshyn
comments.body:
The only way I managed to get durations working properly is by constructing the following date object as a value:
```javascript
cell.value = new Date(
    Date.UTC(1899, 11, 30, hours, minutes, seconds, 0)
)
```
I believe it is something to do with how Excel stores dates (1900 / 1904 mode).

comments.author: frenzymind
